segments.text: In the eighth month, in the second year of Darius, came the word of the LORD unto Zechariah, the son of Berechiah, the son of Iddo the prophet, saying,
The LORD hath been sore displeased with your fathers.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Therefore say thou unto them,
Turn ye unto me, and I will turn unto you
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Be ye not as your fathers, unto whom the former prophets have cried, Turn ye now from your evil ways, and from your evil doings
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: but they did not hear, nor hearken unto me
Your fathers, where are they? and the prophets, do they live for ever?
But my words and my statutes, which I commanded my servants the prophets, did they not take hold of your fathers?
Like as the LORD of hosts thought to do unto us, according to our ways, and according to our doings, so hath he dealt with us.
segments.speaker: Fathers

segments.text: Upon the four and twentieth day of the eleventh month, which is the month Sebat, in the second year of Darius, came the word of the LORD unto Zechariah, the son of Berechiah, the son of Iddo the prophet, saying,
I saw by night, and behold a man riding upon a red horse, and he stood among the myrtle trees that were in the bottom; and behind him were there red horses, speckled, and white.
O my lord, what are these?
segments.speaker: Zechariah

segments.text: I will shew thee what these be.
segments.speaker: Angel

segments.text: These are they whom the LORD hath sent to walk to and fro through the earth.
segments.speaker: Man among myrtle trees

segments.text: We have walked to and fro through the earth, and, behold, all the earth sitteth still, and is at rest.
segments.speaker: Horsemen

segments.text: O LORD of hosts, how long wilt thou not have mercy on Jerusalem and on the cities of Judah, against which thou hast had indignation these threescore and ten years?
segments.speaker: Angel of the LORD

segments.text: The LORD answered the angel that talked with me with good words and comfortable words.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So the angel that communed with me said unto me, Cry thou, saying,
I am jealous for Jerusalem and for Zion with a great jealousy.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I am very sore displeased with the heathen that are at ease: for I was but a little displeased, and they helped forward the affliction.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I am returned to Jerusalem with mercies: my house shall be built in it, and a line shall be stretched forth upon Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Cry yet, saying,
My cities through prosperity shall yet be spread abroad; and the LORD shall yet comfort Zion, and shall yet choose Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then lifted I up mine eyes, and saw, and behold four horns.
What be these?
segments.speaker: Zechariah

segments.text: These are the horns which have scattered Judah, Israel, and Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: Angel

segments.text: And the LORD shewed me four carpenters.
What come these to do?
segments.speaker: Zechariah

segments.text: These are the horns which have scattered Judah, so that no man did lift up his head: but these are come to fray them, to cast out the horns of the Gentiles, which lifted up their horn over the land of Judah to scatter it.
segments.speaker: Angel
